title: Elï؟½anore.
content:
آ آ آ آ I.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The forest flowers are faded all,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The winds complain, the snow-flakes fall,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Elï؟½anore!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I turn to thee, as to a bower: -
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Thou breathest beauty like a flower,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Thou smilest like a happy hour,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Elï؟½anore!


آ آ آ آ II.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I turn to thee. I bless afar
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Thy name, which is my guiding-star,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Elï؟½anore!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And yet, ah God! when thou art here
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I faint, I hold my breath for fear.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Art thou some phantom wandering near,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Elï؟½anore?


آ آ آ آ III.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Oh, take me to thy bosom fair;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Oh, cover me with thy golden hair,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Elï؟½anore!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ There let me lie when I am dead,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Those morning beams about me spread,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The glory of thy face o'erhead,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Elï؟½anore!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Eric Mackay, titled "Elï؟½anore.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
